title: 4 Patriots roster positional battles to follow during OTAs
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Patriots begin OTAs with key positional battles at offensive line, running back, linebacker, and wide receiver positions.
Caleb Lomu, a 2026 first-round pick, is competing for a spot on the offensive line, possibly replacing veteran Morgan Moses.
The running back competition is open with the team's investment in Myles Montgomery and Jam Miller aiming for key roles.

title: Former Stoughton cop accused of killing Sandra Birchmore denied bail pending trial
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Former Stoughton cop Matthew Farwell denied bail as a flight risk before his October 5 federal trial for murder.
Judge M. Page Kelley cites strong evidence, alleging Farwell killed Sandra Birchmore in 2021 to cover up past crimes.
DNA tests show Farwell was not the father of Birchmore's unborn child; trial evidence exclusion requests are pending.

title: Joe Mazzulla named NBA Coach of the Year for 2025-26 season
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Joe Mazzulla named NBA Coach of the Year for 2025-26, first Celtics coach since 1980 to win.
Celtics finished second in Eastern Conference with 56-26 record, overcoming injuries and roster changes.
Mazzulla praised staff, calling for award recognition, despite first-round playoff exit against Philadelphia.

title: New Hampshire mother and son found dead in suspected murder-suicide
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Dover police discovered Eileen Shevelin, 78, and Thomas Wallace, 57, dead in their home on Dover Point Road.
Authorities are investigating the case as a possible murder-suicide; Wallace had a gunshot wound to the head.
The Office of the Chief Medical Examiner in Concord, N.H., will conduct autopsies on Wednesday as part of the investigation.

title: Red Sox get good news on Roman Anthony, Garrett Crochet’s injuries
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Roman Anthony is progressing in his recovery from a sprained ligament in his right ring finger, showing improvement.
Garrett Crochet threw an inning of live batting practice, indicating progress in his recuperation from left shoulder inflammation.
Red Sox interim manager Chad Tracy confirmed positive updates for Anthony and Crochet, with both players showing signs of recovery.

title: Mass. police respond after ‘body’ reported in truck, turns out to be a mannequin
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Wakefield police were alerted to a potential dead body in a truck, which was actually a mannequin.
A 911 caller mistook the dummy used for fire and rescue training for a deceased person.
Police confirmed no dead body was found, clearing up misinformation reported by others.

title: Man spent hours between wall and elevator shaft at Fields Corner MBTA station, police say
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: A man was trapped between an elevator shaft and a wall at Fields Corner MBTA station, Dorchester.
Transit Police stated the man broke into a secure area seeking shelter; he will face trespassing charges.
The individual was rescued by firefighters at around 3:30 p.m. after witnesses alerted authorities.

title: 2-year-old bitten by raccoon at Mass. town beach
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A 2-year-old boy was bitten by a raccoon at Wayland Town Beach, requiring stitches and rabies vaccinations.
Police continue to search for the raccoon and have increased monitoring at Wayland Town Beach after the attack.
Authorities advised avoiding wild animals and stressed urgent medical attention following any bites or scratches.

title: 3 Boston cheese shops perfect for the ultimate summer picnic spread
author: Kelly Egan
bullets: American Provisions in South Boston offers artisan cheeses, charcuterie, and picnic boxes, with popular options like Campagnier and Drunken Goat.
Bacco's Wine and Cheese in Back Bay specializes in international cheeses and offers pre-made picnic box sets and sandwiches.
Crane River Cheese Club in Beacon Hill features seasonal New England cheeses and curated Cheese Club Kits for picnics.

title: Boston to scale back Open Newbury Street events this year, Wu says
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Boston plans fewer Open Newbury Street events in 2026 due to a busy schedule of major city events.
Major happenings like World Cup-related events and the city's 250th anniversary celebrations contribute to the reduced schedule.
Open Streets events in other areas like Jamaica Plain and Allston/Brighton are set from August to October.
